parentQuestion: Which of the following are survival needs of the body?

scaffolds.q: Cells need certain things to stay alive.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Without these essentials, cells die and so does the organism.

scaffolds.q: OXYGEN is needed for cellular respiration to produce ATP (energy).
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Without oxygen, brain cells start dying within 4-6 minutes.

scaffolds.q: WATER is essential - the body is about 60% water.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Water is the solvent for all biochemical reactions. No water = no life.

scaffolds.q: NUTRIENTS provide energy and building materials for cells.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Carbs, proteins, fats, vitamins, minerals - all essential.

scaffolds.q: ATMOSPHERIC PRESSURE is needed for breathing and gas exchange.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Without proper pressure, gases won't move in and out of lungs properly.

scaffolds.q: Growth and reproduction are important, but NOT immediate survival needs.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! You can survive without growing or reproducing. You can't survive without oxygen.

scaffolds.q: The IMMEDIATE survival needs: oxygen, water, nutrients, atmospheric pressure.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! These are what you need RIGHT NOW to stay alive.

scaffolds.q: Survival needs = nutrients, water, atmospheric pressure, and oxygen.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Not growth (can stop and survive), not movement (can be still and survive).

scaffolds.q: The answer is 'nutrients, water, atmospheric pressure, and oxygen.'
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Growth and reproduction are NOT immediate survival needs.
